analysis.problem: Inequitable access to quality education and cybersecurity vulnerabilities in digital learning platforms are undermining student outcomes and data safety.
analysis.root_cause: Systemic underinvestment in equitable education infrastructure and lack of standardized cybersecurity protocols in schools and edtech platforms.
leverage_points: Policy makers and education funding bodies

proposed_actions.action: Implement universal digital literacy programs with a focus on marginalized communities
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase literacy rates among English learners by 25% within 3 years

proposed_actions.action: Establish mandatory cybersecurity standards for all educational software platforms
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce data breaches in schools by 50% within 2 years

evidence: The Pandemic Hindered English Learners' Literacy. This Ohio District Is Turning the Tide.: 'We want to help the students continue to thrive, and really everything that we're thinking about with our student services is equitable learning ...'

Latest Canvas Attack Shows Schools Still Struggle With Cybersecurity: 'A cyberattack against one of the world’s largest digital education platforms has forced attention onto the vulnerability of U.S. schools’ data.'
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the broader issue of digital divide and systemic inequities in education, which affect not only student outcomes but also the security and sustainability of digital infrastructure.
analysis.summary: Inequitable access to education and cybersecurity vulnerabilities are threatening student outcomes and data safety, requiring targeted policy and technical interventions.

raw_items.title: Global education

raw_items.title: Literacy
raw_items.summary: Literacy is the ability to read and write, and illiteracy is the inability to read and write.
